meta_title: For Freelancers: analytics setup to Solve can't deploy apps (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't deploy apps. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: Analytics Setup to Solve Deployment Issues
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with deployment failures. Learn how to implement robust analytics setups using Antigravity and Claude Code to monitor and ship faster.
content.answer_block: Deployment failures often stem from blind spots in your production environment. By integrating lightweight, event-driven analytics, you gain observability into runtime errors and user behavior. This tutorial guides you through instrumenting your codebase to catch failures before they become critical, ensuring your client projects ship reliably and scale efficiently.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks crafting the perfect feature, only to hit a wall the moment you push to production. The silence from the server is deafening, and your client is waiting. Deployment anxiety isn't just a lack of skill; it's a lack of visibility. When you don't know why your build is failing, you're flying blind, wasting billable hours on trial-and-error debugging.

But the best freelancers don't guess—they observe. By embedding a telemetry-first mindset into your development workflow, you transform your app from a 'black box' into a transparent engine. Using modern tools like Antigravity and Claude Code, we’ll move from deployment chaos to a predictable, professional delivery pipeline that impresses clients and secures your reputation.

steps.title: Define Telemetry Strategy
steps.description: Before writing code, define the critical success events. We need to track build triggers, deployment status, and runtime exceptions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Lead DevOps Engineer. Create a lightweight telemetry schema for a SaaS deployment pipeline that tracks build start, build success, build failure, and runtime error counts. Output in JSON format.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Generate telemetry schema for deployment tracking'

steps.title: Configure Antigravity Provider
steps.description: Initialize the Antigravity SDK within your project structure to act as the primary sink for your deployment events.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate integration for the Antigravity SDK in a TypeScript/Node.js environment to capture custom deployment events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add-dependency @antigravity/sdk && claude run --task 'Initialize Antigravity logger'

steps.title: Instrument Deployment Hooks
steps.description: Hook into your CI/CD process to send automated signals upon every build attempt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a bash script that triggers an Antigravity event at the start and end of a 'npm run build' command, capturing exit codes.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Wrap build command with Antigravity event reporting'

steps.title: Implement Error Boundary Catchers
steps.description: Ensure that runtime crashes trigger an event report before the process terminates.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an error-handling middleware that logs uncaught exceptions to the Antigravity dashboard with stack trace context.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --task 'Add global error boundary with event logging'

steps.title: Final Integration Audit
steps.description: Use Claude Code to verify that all event streams are correctly piped to your dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current project implementation and confirm all telemetry points are correctly connected to the Antigravity API key.

pro_tips: Always log the 'environment' flag (staging vs production) in every event to avoid polluting your production metrics with dev-test noise.
Use sampling for high-frequency events to keep your analytics costs low without losing statistical significance.
Set up a 'heartbeat' event that fires every 5 minutes; if it stops, you'll know your server is down before the client reports it.
Wrap your third-party API calls in an analytics decorator to track latency, which is often the silent killer of deployment performance.
Store deployment metadata (git hash, branch name) as tags in your events to correlate specific code changes with production stability.
resources: Observability Best Practices for Freelancers: https://observability.dev/guide
content.next_steps: Now that your telemetry is live, set up automated Slack alerts for any event tagged with 'error' or 'failed' to achieve full production peace of mind.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting deployment bugs dictate your schedule. Take control of your infrastructure and start shipping with the confidence of a lead engineer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
